test.name: Required-fields request should route to validate-schema
test.description: User asks whether all required fields in research.json are filled in — that's structural / schema-level validation, not genealogical impossibility detection. Belongs to validate-schema (which uses the validate_research_schema MCP tool). Tests the boundary the brief calls out between check-warnings (impossibilities) and validate-schema (well-formedness).

input.user_message: Make sure all the required fields in research.json are filled in and the file is well-formed.

negative.explanation: The user is asking about structural integrity of research.json (required fields, well-formedness), not about whether the data itself is biologically or temporally possible. validate-schema owns structural checks; check-warnings owns semantic-impossibility checks. The skill's own description explicitly says 'Do NOT use for schema validation (use validate-schema)'.

judge_context: Should NOT invoke person_warnings — the prompt is about file structure, not about a person's data integrity
Should route to validate-schema (or whatever skill owns the validate_research_schema MCP tool)
If the skill is invoked anyway, it should hand off cleanly to validate-schema without running the warning checks
